<<Translated Summary>>

English Abstract:
The objective of this paper is to explore how metacognitive processes can be used in order to improve decision-making. The methodology for this research involves a systematic analysis of data presented within the context of cognitive science and behavioral economics, as well as an evaluation of the effectiveness of different strategies implemented by human agents with respect to their ability to adaptively optimize their own behavior based on new information provided through feedback loops from other agents in order to achieve optimal outcomes.
Theoretical framework: The research aims at exploring how metacognitive abilities can be used for improving decision-making processes, and the evaluation of different strategies implemented by human agents with respect to their ability to adaptively optimize their own behavior based on new information provided through feedback loops from other agents in order to achieve optimal outcomes.
Theoretical framework: The research aims at exploring how metacognitive abilities can be used for improving decision-making processes, and the evaluation of different strategies implemented by human agents with respect to their ability to adaptively optimize their own behavior based on new information provided through feedback loops from other agents in order to achieve optimal outcomes.
